tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of renal impairment (elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria), hypoglycemia (low glucose, low HbA1c), and mild metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). Urinalysis reveals protein, glucose, ketones, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though diabetes control appears excessive. No signs of urinary infection. Recommend urgent clinical correlation for renal function, hypoglycemia risk, and further nephrology evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
